roles.title: Sourcing Specialist - Fleet & Travel
roles.description:
LOCATION - Lisbon, Portugal

HOURS - Full-time (40 hours)

JOB SUMMARY

The Sourcing Specialist is responsible for:

• Supporting sourcing and contracting initiatives to ensure the company’s operational sourcing needs are met, while optimizing price and quality.

• Collaborating with the vendor/supplier during the sourcing process and resolving problems relating to Fleet & Travel operations, pricing or quality/performance.

• Maintaining vendor and contract data, Procurement analysis and documentation, to ensure process compliance.

PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITIES

• Conduct assigned sourcing activities using best practices to meet customers’ quality and delivery requirements at the lowest TCO, while achieving maximum overall cost-effectiveness (data collection, documentation, compliance).

• Support the full contractual review process from negotiating commercial clauses to liaising with the legal team to obtain full legal compliance and minimize risk exposure.

• Prepares regular reporting for analyzing trends, spend patterns, out of policy spend.

• Collaborate with cross-functional team to source, request quotes, manage contracts’ lifecycle.

• Work with suppliers and internal departments to promptly resolve potential disruption or issues and to ensure business continuity via a continuous and secure supply of services.

• Respond to internal and external requests for information and guidance.

• Help document processes via SOPs’ documents for knowledge sharing and process standardization.

• Maintain the highest ethics and standards in conducting procurement activities

SKILLS REQUIRED

• Advanced analytical skills, with the ability to create reports and conduct cost analyses

• Strong problem-solving skills and ability to prioritize multiple projects

• Ability to effectively persuade others to listen, commit and act on a new approach

• Strong organizational, risk management and negotiation skills

• Superior program and project management skills

• Excellent written and oral communication skills

• Ability to work well in a fast-paced, team-driven environment

• Familiarity with sourcing and procurement tools: ERP (SAP preferred), S2P platforms, spend cubes, saving tracking tools

• Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office required

• Complete command of the English language, Portuguese as a plus

QUALIFICATIONS

• Bachelor’s degree or equivalent, a Master’s degree in Procurement, Supply Chain or similar discipline is a plus

• 3 to 5 years of minimum experience in purchasing or sourcing role

• Excellent proficiency in Microsoft Outlook, Word and Excel

• Advanced MRP/ERP knowledge, as well as a working knowledge of supplier contract maintenance and sourcing techniques is highly preferred

• Business spend management, SAP and S2P suite (Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua) knowledge would be beneficial

WHAT IS ON OFFER?

As a colleague at Scandinavian Tobacco Group, you will receive a comprehensive compensation package. Our benefits differ across regions; our recruiting team will be able to give more details.

OUR HIRING PROCESS

Before we enter a hiring process, your job application will be reviewed by a member of our recruiting team. Your application will be reviewed in line with the hiring competencies we have for the role you have applied for. If your experience aligns with the requirements, you will enter our hiring process which will consist of the following:

• Recruiter Screen

• Team Interview

• Behavioral Assessment

• Leadership Interview

ABOUT SCANDINAVIAN TOBACCO GROUP

At Scandinavian Tobacco Group, we always encourage you to craft the career that makes you more. We have a rich heritage that dates back 250 years and have been operating as a Scandinavian Tobacco Group since 1961. We operate in over 100 countries, employing over 10,000 people. We offer a diverse portfolio of iconic brands ranging from Signature, Cohiba, Macanudo, CAO, Partagas and most recently branched into smokeless tobacco through our recent acquisition of XQS. We are on a mission to focus on sustainability and minimizing our environmental footprint. We are committed to creating a culture that allows us to blend our strengths, to go far it takes a winning team, and this is why we pride ourselves on sharing our skills, knowledge and expertise to push each other forward.

Scandinavian Tobacco Group Talent Team

Scandinavian Tobacco Group is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

roles.title: Senior Tax Analyst
roles.description:
Location: Carnaxide, Lisbon, Portugal

Employment Type: Full-time (40 hours per week)

About the role:

Here at STG we are searching for a talented and experienced Senior Tax Analyst, who will be responsible for executing all day-to-day tax activities as per the tax checklist. This role involves performing all tax calculations, preparing returns and declarations, and filing with tax authorities.

Key Responsibilities:

• Preparation of Corporate Income Tax prepayments, estimations and declarations for different EU countries, with preference for Denmark, Belgium, Netherlands, UK and Germany

• Validation, preparation and reporting of Withholding taxes for different countries - is a plus.

• Support on preparation and revision of indirect tax returns (VAT, Intrastat, Packaging Tax, etc.) and statical reports across relevant entities.

• Experience in preparing and reporting the packaging Tax, Extended Producer Responsibility, Single Use Tax - is a plus.

• Support to other teams and to other team members on Tax queries and propose best practices in Tax

• Assist in gathering, reconciling, and validating tax data extracted from SAP S/4 Hana to ensure accurate tax reporting.

• Introduce controls and verifications to declarations and to reports.

• Monitor tax compliance calendars to ensure all filings and payments are completed on time.

• Support the resolution of discrepancies identified in tax reviews, coordinating with Finance, AP, AR, and other internal stakeholders.

• Preparation of responses and relevant documentation for inquiries from auditors or tax authorities.

• Research tax rules, rates, and legislation changes using internal knowledge bases or external regulatory updates.

• Maintain clear, organized tax documentation in digital archive for audit readiness, including filing proofs, working papers, and payment proofs.

• Support internal controls execution related to tax processes, ensuring tasks are completed in a timely and accurate manner.

Bring ideas of improvement initiatives to enhance tax processes, including automation opportunities and SAP optimization.

Expectations with the support of the Tax supervisor:

• Collaborate closely with tax analysts and tax supervisor to ensure smooth execution of monthly, quarterly and yearly compliance cycles.

• Communicate effectively with cross‑functional teams (Record to Report, Procure to Pay, Quote to Cash, Treasury) to resolve tax‑related issues and align procedures.

• Escalate critical issues, delays, or risks to the Tax Lead or Senior Analyst in a timely manner.

• Support onboarding activities for new team members by sharing documentation and demonstrating agreed procedures when needed.

• Participate in team meetings, training sessions, and knowledge‑sharing initiatives within the Tax / Finance organization and support the preparation of tax training.

Qualifications and Experience:

Required:
• Experience: 6-7 years in tax compliance, planning, and operations with VAT and corporate income tax experience
• Understanding of local and international tax laws, especially in the European Union
• Familiarity with tax software; experience with SAP S/4 HANA is an advantage.
• Optional but sought after: tax experience in Denmark, Northern EU countries and/or Italy
• Functional leadership skills
• Positive posture and can do attitude, «builder» profile
• Strong communication skills
• Education: BSc/BA (Hons) in Accounting, Finance, Taxation, or a related field

What We Offer

At Scandinavian Tobacco Group, you will join a dynamic team that values heritage and innovation. We provide a comprehensive and competitive compensation package tailored to your location. Our benefits program is designed to support your personal and professional well-being. You will be part of a global company with a rich 250-year history and a commitment to sustainability and reducing environmental impact.

We cultivate a culture where knowledge-sharing and teamwork are core, enabling everyone to progress together. This role offers the opportunity to develop your leadership career within a company that embraces diversity, inclusion, and continuous improvement.

Our Hiring Process

Your application will be carefully reviewed by our recruitment team for alignment with the role's competencies. Qualified candidates will proceed through a multi-step selection process including:

• Recruiter screening interview

• Hiring Manager interview

• Behavioral assessment

• Leadership interview

About Scandinavian Tobacco Group

Scandinavian Tobacco Group is a leader in the tobacco industry, operating in over 100 countries with more than 10,000 employees worldwide. Our extensive portfolio includes iconic brands such as Signature, Cohiba, Macanudo, CAO, and Partagas. Recently, we expanded into smokeless tobacco with the acquisition of XQS. We are committed to sustainable practices and creating a supportive, inclusive workplace where every individual can thrive.

Please note: This is a direct recruitment conducted exclusively by Scandinavian Tobacco Group. We do not accept applications from recruitment agencies and will not provide compensation for unsolicited resumes.

roles.title: Junior Collections Officer
roles.description:
Location: Carnaxide, Lisbon, Portugal

Employment Type: Full-time (40 hours per week)

About the role:

The Junior Collections Officer will be responsible for managing and matching receipts accurately, constant monitoring of unapplied cash receipts, negotiating settlements with customers. The Junior Collections Officer will also follow up with sales team to ensure timely collections from customer

· Take ownership of learning and development, seeking guidance from the team lead

· Closely co-ordinate with the Sales team to track customer collections and assist the Sales team to have focused efforts for customer follow-ups

· Ensure timely completion and submission of all reports to enable the collection lead to evaluate process performance.

Performance & Efficiency:

· Demonstrate proficient problem-solving abilities and negotiation skills to resolve payment issues

· Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or similar

· Knowledge of SAP 4 HANA (knowledge of FSCM module in SAP preferred)

roles.title: Reporting & Analytics Lead
roles.description:
Reporting & Analytics Lead

Location: Carnaxide, Lisbon, Portugal

Company: STG

Employment Type: Full-time (40 hours per week)

About the Role

As the Reporting & Analytics Lead at STG, you will play a pivotal role in managing and evolving our business controlling, data & insights, and manufacturing costing functions. This leadership position requires strong expertise in finance and business acumen, as well as a passion for driving process excellence and delivering strategic insights. Reporting to senior management, you will lead a diverse team of specialists, empowering them to optimize financial performance and support data-driven decision-making across the organization.

Key Responsibilities

• Team Leadership and Development: Lead, mentor, and inspire a team of 7 professionals including business controllers (commercial and operational), business intelligence analysts, and costing process analysts. Foster a collaborative culture that values continuous learning, open communication, and high performance. Conduct regular performance assessments and provide constructive feedback to support career growth.

• Financial Controlling and Reporting: Oversee the accuracy and quality of financial reporting related to commercial areas (brands, markets) and operational segments (stock, manufacturing costs). Collaborate closely with finance business partners to enhance controls and streamline processes, aiming for greater automation and reliability in financial insights.

• Support to Finance Business Partners: Prepare comprehensive business summary reports, forecasts, and ad hoc analyses using tools such as Jedox, enabling finance teams to effectively monitor financial health and operational efficiencies. Conduct detailed variance analyses including purchase price variances, inventory management, and procurement savings.

• Business Analytics: Utilize advanced analytics capabilities leveraging Power BI and SAP platforms to develop meaningful and actionable financial reports. Your insights will facilitate timely and informed decision-making at various organizational levels.

• Manufacturing Costing Management: Lead standard cost calculations and costing runs while collaborating with global finance partners to refine and maintain costing policies. Ensure costing practices align with organizational standards and reflect current business realities.

• Process Improvement and Innovation: Identify opportunities to enhance efficiency, scope, and accuracy within the reporting and analytics function. Implement best practices and technology enhancements to promote continuous improvement and scalability in a growth-oriented phase.

Qualifications and Experience

• 8 to 12 years of progressive experience in Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A), Controlling, or Finance Business Partnering roles.

• Proven leadership experience with a track record of managing and developing high-performing teams.

• Strong technical proficiency in SAP (preferably S/4 Hana) environment and advanced Excel skills.

• Deep analytical capabilities with the ability to interpret complex financial data and transform it into clear, impactful business insights.

• Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, comfortable engaging with diverse stakeholders across multiple departments.

• A proactive, curious mindset with a dedication to enhancing reporting and analytics processes during periods of organizational growth.

• Preferred educational background includes MSc in Accounting & Finance, MBA in Finance, or professional certifications such as CMA, ACCA, or CPA.

What We Offer

At Scandinavian Tobacco Group, you will join a dynamic team that values heritage and innovation. We provide a comprehensive and competitive compensation package tailored to your location. Our benefits program is designed to support your personal and professional well-being. You will be part of a global company with a rich 250-year history and a commitment to sustainability and reducing environmental impact.

We cultivate a culture where knowledge-sharing and teamwork are core, enabling everyone to progress together. This role offers the opportunity to develop your leadership career within a company that embraces diversity, inclusion, and continuous improvement.

Our Hiring Process

Your application will be carefully reviewed by our recruitment team for alignment with the role's competencies. Qualified candidates will proceed through a multi-step selection process including:

• Recruiter screening interview

• Hiring Manager interview

• Behavioral assessment

• Leadership interview

roles.title: Data Management Analyst
roles.description:
SAP Data Management Analyst – Finance

At STG, we always encourage you to craft the career that makes you more. Working for one of our world-class brands means being part of a great working environment and collaborating with people from all corners of the world on a product you are proud of.

Be part of something transformative in Lisbon! Scandinavian Tobacco Group is establishing our Solution Delivery Organization (SDO) - a centre of excellence that will shape our digital future.

As a leader in the cigar industry, we're building an innovative tech hub where bright minds create impactful solutions. In our modern, collaborative space, your expertise will drive digital transformation and technological advancement.

Role Details

• Location: Lisbon, Portugal

• Working Hours: 40 hours per week

• Contract Type: Direct employment

What can you expect as a SAP Data Management Analyst at Scandinavian Tobacco Group? Your primary tasks will be:

In this role, you will be responsible for the governance, quality, and maintenance of financial data within SAP and related systems, ensuring accurate, consistent, and compliant financial information across the enterprise. You will work closely with Finance, IT, and data stakeholders to support SAP initiatives, master data processes, and continuous data quality improvements.

What will you be responsible for?

• Support MDM and SAP system upgrades, data migrations, and integration initiatives related to financial data.

• Monitor financial data quality, perform root‑cause analyses, and drive remediation actions.

• Collaborate closely with Finance stakeholders to ensure data accuracy, completeness, and compliance with internal and external standards.

• Manage financial data requests, changes, and periodic maintenance activities.

• Provide guidance on data‑related issues, resolve conflicts, and enforce adherence to data governance policies.

• Track, analyze, and report on key data quality metrics for financial data.

• Stay up to date with data governance, SAP, and financial reporting best practices.

What skills and experience will be required?

• Experience in data quality management or data maintenance roles, ideally with a focus on financial data.

• Solid understanding of financial data and its role in reporting, compliance, and analysis.

• Familiarity with financial systems and data management practices, particularly SAP FI‑CO.

• Knowledge of data standards, data governance principles, data quality practices, and financial reporting requirements.

• Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills with high attention to detail.

• Excellent communication skills and fluency in English (spoken and written).

• Ability to collaborate effectively with cross‑functional and international stakeholders.

• A proactive mindset and strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity.

How to Apply:

If you are ready to be a part of our exciting journey at STG, please, apply by including your resume and highlighting your experience, as well as any other relevant information you would like to share with us.

Thank you for considering this exciting opportunity.

roles.title: Senior SAP Data Engineer
roles.description:
Senior SAP Data Engineer (Datasphere, BDC, SAC & Cloud Data Platform)

About the Role

We are looking for a Senior SAP Data Engineer to take a key role in building and scaling our cloud-native data platform with SAP at its core.

You will work across SAP data integration, semantic modeling, and analytics while contributing to a broader modern data ecosystem leveraging SAP Datasphere, SAP Business Data Cloud, and SAP Analytics Cloud, alongside Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI.

This role sits at the intersection of SAP and modern data platforms, helping transform SAP data into trusted, business-ready data products that power reporting, planning, and advanced analytics across the enterprise.

• Location: Lisbon, Portugal

• Working hours: Full-time

• Contract type: Direct employmen - 1 year contract

What You’ll Do

• Design and implement data models and data products in SAP Datasphere

• Build and optimize cloud-native data pipelines from SAP S/4HANA and other SAP/non-SAP sources

• Integrate SAP data into the broader cloud data platform (Databricks, Microsoft Fabric)

• Work with SAP-native integration capabilities within SAP Business Data Cloud

• Develop analytical datasets consumed by SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and Power BI

• Implement delta handling, data harmonization, and scalable semantic layers

• Ensure high data quality, consistency, and governance across SAP and enterprise data assets

• Collaborate with business stakeholders (finance, supply chain, sales) to translate requirements into scalable data solutions

• Contribute to evolving our modern, cloud-native data architecture and platform standards

What We’re Looking For

• 5+ years of experience in data engineering, analytics engineering, or SAP data modeling

• Strong hands-on experience with SAP Datasphere

• Experience integrating data from SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC

• Solid understanding of SAP data models (FI/CO, SD, MM, etc.)

• Experience with semantic modeling and data product design

• Strong SQL skills and understanding of data transformation logic

• Experience working in modern cloud data ecosystems (e.g., Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, Azure)

• Familiarity with Power BI or other BI tools

Nice to Have

• Experience with SAP Business Data Cloud (or similar SAP cloud integration frameworks)

• Hands-on experience with Databricks (Spark, Delta Lake) or Microsoft Fabric

• Experience with end-to-end data pipelines beyond SAP (ELT/ETL frameworks)

• Familiarity with Lakehouse architectures and medallion design patterns

• Experience with data governance and master data (e.g., SAP MDG/MDQ)

• Knowledge of planning processes (finance or supply chain)

Why Join Us?

We are building a modern, cloud-native data platform that combines SAP technologies (Datasphere, Business Data Cloud, SAC) with a broader ecosystem including Databricks, Microsoft Fabric, and Power BI.

Our goal is to move beyond traditional SAP reporting into a scalable, product-driven data platform, where SAP data is seamlessly integrated with enterprise data to power advanced analytics, AI, and decision-making.

You’ll be part of a team redefining how data is used—bridging SAP and the modern data stack to create real business impact.

How to Apply:

If you are ready to be a part of our exciting journey at STG, please, apply by including your resume and highlighting your experience, as well as any other relevant information you would like to share with us.

Thank you for considering this exciting opportunity.

Scandinavian Tobacco Group Talent Team

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

roles.title: SAP ERP Support - Plan-to-Deliver (SAP PP Consultant)
roles.description:
JOB TITLE - SAP ERP Support – Plan-to-Deliver (SAP PP Consultant)

LOCATION – Dominican Republic

MODE – Hybrid

HOUR – 40 hours

At STG, we always encourage you to craft the career that makes you more.
Working for one of our world-class brands means being part of a great working environment and collaborating with people from all corners of the world on a product you are proud of.

Summary:
We have an incredible opportunity for a P2D Process Professional (SAP PP Consultant) to join our IT team. As a P2D Process Professional, you will be responsible for supporting and optimizing Plan to Deliver processes leveraging and supporting SAP S/4 HANA solutions and ensuring efficient and compliant P2D operations. This position offers a dynamic and collaborative work environment where your contributions will directly impact on the company's operational excellence and regulatory adherence.

What can you expect as a P2D Professional at Scandinavian Tobacco Group?
You will analyze the current P2D processes in detail, identifying potential risks and areas for improvement. Leveraging your expertise, you will recommend and implement robust control measures to mitigate identified risks and optimize operations. Your role will also involve continuous interaction with cross-functional teams to drive process improvements, enhance compliance, and ensure that our P2D operations are efficient.

Your primary tasks will include the following:

• Supporting current P2D processes, identifying risks, and recommending control measures and process improvements

• Participating in Roll Out projects throughout all regions and countries within STG ecosystem

• Implementing best practices for P2D processes, ensuring compliance with internal policies and external regulations

• Configuring and customizing SAP solutions (especially SAP PP) to support P2D processes

• Collaborating with different teams across the company to streamline P2D processes

• Providing end-user support for P2D processes

• Monitoring process automation and continuous improvement initiatives within P2D operations

• Supporting process automation and continuous monitoring initiatives within P2D operations

• Developing training materials and conducting end-user training on P2D processes and SAP solutions

• Staying updated on P2D process trends and internal requirements

Your areas of knowledge and expertise (that matters the most for this role):

• Bachelor’s or Master's degree in Information Technology, SAP, and/or a related field

• At least 3+ years of experience in supporting and optimizing Plan to Deliver processes, mainly throughout SAP PP module. It will be highly appreciated knowledge on SAP SCM/LE/EWM modules

• Experience working with SAP, and previous expertise in customizing, configuring and supporting SAP solutions around P2D processes

• Ability to analyze processes, identify risks, and implement control measures and process improvements

• Knowledge of process automation techniques and continuous improvement methodologies

• Strong understanding of P2D processes

• Excellent communication and training skills to provide end-user support

• English proficiency

What happens next?

• You Apply - Join a world-leading manufacturer of cigars and pipe tobacco

• We carefully review your application (over a nice cup of coffee).

• The important thing to know is that we process applications on an ongoing basis, so we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible

• If we are a match, you’ll receive an email or a call from our HR team

• And a final note for you to know - We'll get back to you — even if it's a no (for now)

roles.title: IT Application Security Analyst
roles.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE…

The IT Application Security Analyst plays a key role in embedding security by design across the enterprise software development lifecycle (SDLC). This position partners closely with development, DevOps, QA, and IT Operations teams to integrate secure development frameworks, tooling, and practices that strengthen the security, resilience, and compliance of STG’s applications and platforms.

The role focuses on advancing application security maturity by aligning development practices with industry standards such as NIST SSDF and OWASP ASVS, while enabling teams to deliver software securely and efficiently.

WHAT WILL YOU BE RESPONSIBLE FOR?

Secure SDLC & Governance

• Assess and continuously improve SDLC processes, tools, and release workflows from a security perspective.

• Perform gap analyses against secure‑development frameworks including NIST SSDF and OWASP ASVS.

• Define, maintain, and evolve secure development standards and procedures aligned with regulatory requirements such as PCI DSS and CCPA/GDPR.

• Partner with engineering teams to recommend and implement practical security improvements across the SDLC.

Application Security Enablement

• Embed security controls across all SDLC phases, including planning, design, coding, testing, deployment, and maintenance.

• Deliver threat modeling, secure‑design guidance, and application architecture reviews.

• Establish and support secure design and code‑review practices, coaching developers on security best practices.

• Balance security requirements with developer experience and business requirements to reduce friction while increasing security maturity.

AppSec Tooling & Automation

• Implement, operate, and optimize application security tooling including SAST, DAST, and SCA solutions.

• Integrate security tooling (e.g., Snyk, Checkmarx) into CI/CD pipelines to enable automated vulnerability detection.

• Define and enforce security gates or holds at key points within development and release workflows.

• Ensure vulnerability findings are actionable, prioritized, and integrated into remediation processes.

Testing, Monitoring & Vulnerability Management

• Support static, dynamic, and penetration testing activities in partnership with internal and external resources.

• Integrate vulnerability management, continuous monitoring, and remediation tracking into the SDLC.

• Provide application‑security support during security incidents and assist teams with investigation and remediation.

Platform & Architecture Security

• Support secure platform and environment modernization efforts, including container security, OS hardening, and secrets management (e.g., Vault, Azure Key Vault).

• Contribute to application and platform architecture improvements focused on security, stability, and resilience.

REQUIREMENTS:

• 3+ years of experience in Application Security or Software Engineering with a focus on secure development practices.

• Hands‑on experience implementing secure SDLC frameworks such as NIST SSDF and OWASP ASVS.

• Practical experience integrating SAST/DAST tools into CI/CD pipelines and workflows.

• Working knowledge of PCI DSS and privacy regulations (CCPA/GDPR) as they impact software development.

• Strong communication skills with the ability to influence and collaborate with engineering teams.

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

• Experience with container security, image hardening, and secrets management technologies.

• Familiarity with the OWASP Top 10, API security, and modern application security practices.

• Experience coordinating or supporting penetration testing or DAST programs.

• Relevant certifications such as CSSLP, CISSP, GWAPT, GCSA, or similar.

WHAT’S IN AN OFFER?

As a colleague at Scandinavian Tobacco Group, you will receive a comprehensive compensation package as a generous benefits package.

• Comprehensive Health Care, Vision & Dental Plan

• Flexible Spending Account

• Disability Plans

• Basic & Supplemental Life Insurance

• Additional Supplemental Benefits

• Paid Vacation, Paid Time Off (PTO) days, Holidays

• 401(k) Retirement Saving Plan including a generous Company match

This role requires deep expertise in channel-specific strategy development and strong capability to manage complex sales ecosystems across multiple geographies, channels, and markets.

WHAT WILL YOU BE RESPONSIBLE FOR?

• Create and execute a strategic vision for trade and channel marketing, aligning with company goals and market trends.

• Lead creation of unified global trade and channel marketing strategy for Handmade Cigars

• Develop global channel blueprints and frameworks.

• Drive revenue growth through global and local sales initiatives to help the sales teams achieve budget targets.

• Lead market activation & regional execution leadership, including developing and implementing global activation standards

• Analyze market trends, customer feedback, and performance metrics to make informed business decisions.

• Oversee global trade marketing budgets and establish governance processes.

• Champion the adoption of new technologies and best practices to enhance operational efficiency and customer experience.

• Ensure compliance with all company policies, legal requirements, and industry regulations.

• Act as liaison between brand marketing, sales, and other global groups

• Partner with brand marketing and sales to develop and execute promotional campaigns and data-driven strategies.

QUALIFICATIONS

• 10+ years of progressive experience, developing, designing, and implementing marketing strategies across multiple channels and global markets

• Demonstrated ability to build, lead, motivate, and develop high-performing teams.

• Highly collaborative, excellent communicator, strong interpersonal, and problem-solving skills.

• Bachelor’s degree in business, Retail or Hospitality Management, Marketing, or related field (MBA preferred).

• Strong financial acumen, with experience managing budgets with P&L responsibility.

• Experience with omnichannel, wholesale, and retail strategies and digital transformation is highly desirable.

• Ability to travel as needed.

WHAT’S IN AN OFFER?

As a colleague at Scandinavian Tobacco Group, you will receive a comprehensive compensation package as a generous benefits package.

• Comprehensive Health Care, Vision & Dental Plan

• Flexible Spending Account

• Disability Plans

• Basic & Supplemental Life Insurance

• Additional Supplemental Benefits

• Paid Vacation, Paid Time Off (PTO) days, Holidays

roles.title: Business Analyst - Iberia
roles.description:
JOB TITLE – Business Analyst - Iberia

LOCATION – Madrid, Spain

HOURS – 40

ABOUT THE ROLE

At Scandinavian Tobacco Group European Sales Cluster, we are dedicated to utilizing data to drive business success. We are seeking a dynamic and detail-oriented Business Analyst to maximize the value of our data through accurate sales & marketing analysis, actionable insights, and forecasting. This role is pivotal in supporting our sales and marketing teams to accelerate growth and efficiency.

WHAT WILL YOU BE RESPONSIBLE FOR?

• Sales Performance Management: conduct target and performance analyses, monitor campaign performance and KPIs, provide recommendations and actionable insights.

• Sales Excellence: drive value from data sources ensuring sales efficiency, visualize and maintain KPIs in Microsoft Power BI.

• Key User CRM (Salesforce): maintenance, first-line support and coaching of the Field Force.

• Category & Market Analysis: perform category analysis and derive insights for both internal and external stakeholders.

• Project Management: involvement in cross-functional projects.

WHAT SKILLS AND EXPERIENCE WILL BE REQUIRED?

• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Analytics, or a related field.

• 2–5 years in sales operations or a data/insights role; FMCG experience is highly advantageous.

• Native-level Spanish and fluent English required.

• Strong analytical skills with high attention to detail and ability to extract insights from complex data.

• Expertise in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint), CRM systems (Salesforce), and Power BI.

• Strong communication skills with a collaborative and proactive mindset.

WHAT IS ON OFFER?

Scandinavian Tobacco Group offers a comprehensive compensation package. Benefits vary by region and will be discussed during the recruitment process. The salary range for this role is from € 30,000 - € 44000 annually.

OUR HIRING PROCESS

• Recruiter Screen

• Hiring Manager Interview

• Behavioral Assessment

• Leadership Interview

ABOUT SCANDINAVIAN TOBACCO GROUP

Scandinavian Tobacco Group operates in over 100 countries with more than 10,000 employees worldwide. The company owns iconic brands including Cohiba, Macanudo, CAO, Partagas, and XQS. STG focuses on sustainability, collaboration, and long-term growth while maintaining a strong international culture.

roles.title: Inventory Control Coordinator Europe
roles.description:
LOCATION – Germany, Norway or the Netherlands. Remote Position

HOURS – 40 hours/week

POSITION SUMMARY:

Reporting to the Inventory Control Manager, Europe, the position is a role with multi-country responsibilities within Region Europe.

The Inventory Coordinator is responsible and accountable for the completion of administrative and reporting tasks in support of regional inventory control processes, with a critical focus on accuracy and timeliness.

The role will be supporting processes in Europe, where operations may be running in different time zones to where the candidate is located. Because of this, some flexibility and occasional non-standard scheduled support will be necessary.

The candidate is also expected to help cover and manage critical tasks needed during any periods where their line manager or colleague(s) in the same function are out of the office or not readily available.

RESPONSIBILITIES:

• Weekly creation and analysis of inventory reconciliation reports between our subcontracted 3PL service suppliers and our internal ERP systems.

• Creation and maintenance of inventory accuracy reporting on a weekly/monthly basis.

• Timely reporting and resolution of any issues affecting inventory accuracy, some of which require alignment with stakeholders in other functions before correcting.

• Monitoring of creation and booking of Purchase Orders at our subcontracted warehouses.

• Supporting and reporting of yearly Physical Inventory process within subcontracted 3PL service suppliers under our responsibility.

• Preparation and analysis for local market destruction/scrapping process, including documentation and process execution (under customs supervision).

• Support in management and reconciliation of regional “car stock”.

• Other responsibilities as assigned.

KEY RELATIONSHIPS:

• INTERNAL

•

• Warehousing Team, Europe

• Customer Service, Europe

• 3PL Inbound Team o Planning Team

• Commercial Teams as needed, Europe

• EXTERNAL:

• 3PL service suppliers in the region

EDUCATION & EXPERIENCE:

• Completion of a bachelor’s degree, preferably in Accounting or Supply Chain management.

• Previous experience in a manufacturing or operations environment.

SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS:

• Focus on Quality and Accuracy.

• Analytical and able to work independently.

• Competent with all MS Office applications (Excel proficiency is a must!)

• Comfortable working with remote manager.

• Effective communicator with internal and external stakeholders.

• Flexible and a quick learner.

• Experience with ERP systems, currently AX (SAP is preferred!)

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENTS:

• Languages: Fluent written/spoken English required. Additional written/spoken German, Spanish, Italian, French, or Dutch is welcome.

• Travel: Occasional <5% within region, normally within same day.

STG CORE VALUES:

• Empower with Passion: Uphold high standards for our products, people, and communities.

• Blend our Strengths: Leverage our distinct strengths and internal expertise for stronger results.

• Cultivate Courage: Continue to search for better, simpler solutions in order to take on challenges with resiliency.

• Light the Way Forward: Lead our industry towards a more responsible and innovative future, finding new ways to serve our customers and employees.

WHAT IS ON OFFER?

As a colleague at Scandinavian Tobacco Group, you will receive a comprehensive compensation package. Our benefits differ across regions; our recruiting team will be able to give more details.

OUR HIRING PROCESS

Before we enter a hiring process, your job application will be reviewed by a member of our recruiting team. Your application will be reviewed in line with the hiring competencies we have for the role you have applied for. If your experience aligns with the requirements, you will enter our hiring process which will consist of the following:

• Recruiter Screen

• Hiring Manager Interview

• Behavioral Assessment

• Team Interview

ABOUT SCANDINAVIAN TOBACCO GROUP

At Scandinavian Tobacco Group, we always encourage you to craft the career that makes you more. We have a rich heritage that dates back 250 years and have been operating as a Scandinavian Tobacco Group since 1961. We operate in over 100 countries, employing over 10,000 people. We offer a diverse portfolio of iconic brands ranging from Signature, Cohiba, Macanudo, CAO, Partagas and most recently branched into smokeless tobacco through our recent acquisition of XQS. We are on a mission to focus on sustainability and minimizing our environmental footprint. We are committed to creating a culture that allows us to blend our strengths, to go far it takes a winning team, and this is why we pride ourselves on sharing our skills, knowledge and expertise to push each other forward.

HOW TO APPLY:

If you are ready to be a part of our exciting journey at STG, please, apply by including your resume and highlighting your experience, as well as any other relevant information you would like to share with us.

Thank you for considering this exciting opportunity

SCANDINAVIAN TOBACCO GROUP TALENT TEAM

Scandinavian Tobacco Group is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Please be informed that this Direct Search is conducted exclusively by Scandinavian Tobacco Group. We do not accept applications from agencies, and we will not provide compensation for unsolicited CVs.

roles.title: Expedition Supervisor
roles.description:
SUPERVISOR DE EXPEDICION

Esteli, Nicaragua

SOBRE EL ROL:

Tenemos una interesante oportunidad para que un Supervisor de Expedición se incorpore a nuestro equipo. Esta posición tiene como objetivo garantizar que todos los embarques y/o envíos de productos disponibles se realicen en tiempo y forma, cumpliendo con los protocolos y requerimientos de la empresa. Asimismo, será responsable de contribuir al cumplimiento de las normativas del sistema CTPAT.

¿CUÁLES SERÁN TUS RESPONSABILIDADES?

1. Planificación y coordinación de embarques

• Dar seguimiento a la planificación, coordinar la consolidación de envíos, gestión de reserva de contenedores.

2. Gestión operativa y de inventarios

• Control del producto terminado, revisiones de inventarios, cálculo del consumo de materiales.

3. Procesos administrativos y documentación

• Elaboracion y registro de facturación de los envíos, generar y revisión de ordenes de envíos, actualizar proyecciones de contenedores (NIO) y hojas de costos de terceros.

4. Control financiero y soporte a negocios

• Dar soporte a finanzas en cobros de ventas internacionales (Canadá, FTT), confirmar ventas mensuales, revisión de precios de terceros y validar precios en packing list.

5. Cumplimiento y mejora continua

• Garantizar el cumplimiento de las normativas C-TPAT, realización auditorías internas (Lean, EHS).

6. Coordinación transversal

• Mantener comunicación con proveedores, fábricas terceras y áreas internas.

¿QUÉ HABILIDADES Y EXPERIENCIA SE REQUERIRÁN?

• Administración de Empresas, Contaduría pública o Carrera afín.

• Experiencia de Procesos y Procedimientos de Embarque y envíos de producto terminado.

• Dominio del idioma ingles (intermedio)

• Manejo de Inventarios

• Manejo de sistemas BCS, ORACLE, MANHATTAN SCALE, DISCOVERER.

• Manejo de normativas del C-TPAT.

• Manejo de Lean Manufacturing

• Trabajo en alturas.

• Operación de montacargas.

¿QUÉ OFRECEMOS?

Como empleado de Scandinavian Tobacco Group, recibirás un paquete retributivo completo. Nuestras prestaciones varían según la región; nuestro equipo de selección de personal te proporcionará más detalles al respecto.

NUESTRO PROCESO DE SELECCIÓN

Requisitos

Antes de iniciar el proceso de selección, un miembro de nuestro equipo de selección revisará tu solicitud de empleo. Tu solicitud se evaluará en función de los requisitos que exigimos para el puesto al que has solicitado. Si tu experiencia se ajusta a los requisitos, pasarás a formar parte de nuestro proceso de selección, que constará de lo siguiente:

• Selección por parte del reclutador

• Evaluación del comportamiento

• Entrevista con el responsable de contratación

• Entrevista con el superior directo

Acerca de Scandinavian Tobacco Group

En Scandinavian Tobacco Group, siempre te animamos a forjar la carrera profesional que te permita dar lo mejor de ti mismo. Contamos con una rica tradición que se remonta a 250 años atrás y operamos como Scandinavian Tobacco Group desde 1961. Estamos presentes en más de 100 países y contamos con una plantilla de más de 10,000 empleados. Ofrecemos una cartera diversa de marcas icónicas que abarca desde Signature, Cohiba, Macanudo, CAO, Partagas, y recientemente nos hemos diversificado hacia el tabaco sin humo mediante nuestra reciente adquisición de XQS. Nuestra misión es centrarnos en la sostenibilidad y minimizar nuestra huella medioambiental. Estamos comprometidos con la creación de una cultura que nos permita combinar nuestras fortalezas; para llegar lejos se necesita un equipo ganador, y por eso nos enorgullecemos de compartir nuestras habilidades, conocimientos y experiencia para impulsarnos mutuamente.

Le informamos de que esta búsqueda directa la lleva a cabo exclusivamente Scandinavian Tobacco Group. No aceptamos solicitudes de agencias y no ofreceremos compensación alguna por CV no solicitados.

roles.description:

Working for one of our world-class brands means being part of a great working environment and collaborating with people from all corners of the world on a product you are proud of. Scandinavian Tobacco Group Indonesia is seeking a highly skilled and experienced individual to join our dynamic Business Services team as the Head of Finance and Accounting. roles.title: Technieker
roles.description:
Werken voor één van onze merken van wereldklasse betekent dat je deel uitmaakt van een geweldige werkomgeving en dat je kan samenwerken met collega’s uit alle hoeken van de wereld aan een product waar je trots op kan zijn.

Wij zijn op zoek naar een technieker om onze vestigingen in Lummen en Westerlo te versterken. Je rapporteert rechtstreeks aan de technisch teamcoach.

Wat kan je verwachten als technieker bij Scandinavian Tobacco Group

Binnen de productielocaties Lummen en Westerlo worden een grote diversiteit aan machine-made cigars geproduceerd. Het productieproces vindt plaats met (deels)geautomatiseerde productie- en verpakkingsmachines. Als technieker sta je in voor het technisch beheer van de productiemachines en het realiseren van continuïteit van het productieproces. Samen met je collega - techniekers, verbeter je de efficiëntie van onze machines door een gerichte storingsanalyse, effectieve interventies en een zorgzaam preventief onderhoud. De nadruk ligt op het mechanisch, pneumatisch en elektrisch vakgebied en in beperkte mate op besturingselektronica.

Deze functie vereist van jou:

• Je bent in het bezit van een diploma A2 Elektro / Mechanica of gelijkwaardig door ervaring. Basiskennis van elektronica is een pluspunt;

• Je bent stressbestendig.

• Je werkt resultaatgericht

• Je hebt een goed beoordelingsvermogen

• Je houdt rekening met omgevingsfactoren

• Je beschikt over een degelijk probleemoplossend vermogen

Procedure:

• Je solliciteert via https://careers.st-group.com en maak je klaar om de volgende stap in je carrière te zetten bij een wereldleider in de productie van sigaren en pijptabak

• Wij bekijken en beoordelen jouw sollicitatie zorgvuldig. Het is belangrijk om te weten dat we de sollicitaties dagelijks behandelen, dus raden we je aan om jouw sollicitatie zo snel mogelijk in te dienen.

• In het geval dat we geïnteresseerd zijn, mag je van ons HR-team een e-mail of telefoontje verwachten.

• Tot slot: zelfs als er geen match is (op dit moment) langs onze zijde, zal je altijd iets van ons horen.
